summary: Independent read-only QA reviewed the dirty working-tree implementation on feat/m4-client-campaign-planning-ui. The actual diff stays within allowed paths, avoids forbidden paths and scope drift, keeps M4 client state as disposable read-model projection data, submits protocol-typed GameCommand DTOs through the UI callback boundary, and exposes campaign planning, supply/route risks, siege/withdrawal state, AI reasons, and war report/postwar handoff information with Playwright coverage.
reviewed_state: HEAD plus uncommitted writer implementation and writer REVIEW handoff in the working tree.
acceptance_evidence: Path check found 14 changed/untracked paths and all are inside task allowed_paths; none match forbidden_paths.

Static boundary scan found no changed client/ui code importing sim-core, storing WorldState, adding Redux/store authority, owning Pixi DisplayObjects, using nodeIntegration, telemetry, Math.random, Date.now, any, or as unknown as.

packages/client-core/src/index.ts imports M4 command and AI trace types from @monsoon/protocol and builds CreateCampaignObjective, StartCampaignMarch, ApplyM4SiegeChoice, ResolveM4CampaignWithdrawal, and CancelCampaignObjective GameCommandV1 DTOs with expected day/revision.

packages/ui/src/index.tsx keeps M4 commands behind onM4CommandSubmit and renders reason chips for campaign forecast, supply/route, muster, march/siege/withdrawal, AI decision candidates, war report, and postwar candidate methods.
apps/web/src/shell/web-client-shell.tsx records submitted command DTO kind/actor as presentation status only; it does not mutate authoritative simulation state.
tests/e2e/web-shell.spec.ts covers M4 planning, command submission buttons, route/supply risk reasons, siege choice, withdrawal, AI reason display, and war report/postwar handoff rendering.
War report UI displays losses, supply loss, resolved day, postwar handoff candidate/methods, and protocol-provided reason codes without duplicating sim-core outcome formulas.

tests_run.result: Branch feat/m4-client-campaign-planning-ui with expected dirty writer files and untracked writer REVIEW handoff.
Task id/status/route matched M4-CLIENT-CAMPAIGN-PLANNING-UI-001, REVIEW, qa_reviewer; allowed_paths 12, forbidden_paths 22.
Vite web production build passed; existing large chunk warning remained.
5 Playwright chromium tests passed, including M4 campaign planning flow.
Architecture boundaries passed; deterministic sim-runner hashes matched; 33 Vitest files and 237 tests passed; web Vitest passed.
Prettier format check, ESLint, recursive typecheck, and architecture boundary check passed.

decisions: ACCEPT because the implementation satisfies the task acceptance criteria with no blocking correctness, architecture-boundary, path, dependency, or test-evidence issues found.

Accepted the current fixture-backed web shell pattern as consistent with the existing M3 client UI boundary: this task builds client read-model projection and protocol DTO submission controls, not a new live simulation Worker integration layer.
Accepted client-side M4 readiness/reason aggregation as display-only projection over protocol-visible fields, not authoritative simulation or duplicated outcome/supply/siege formulas.
risks: The web shell still uses bootstrapped fixture/read-model snapshots and an onM4CommandSubmit status callback rather than a live Worker submit/CommandResult path. This is consistent with the current client shell pattern but should be replaced by live SimulationPort wiring in a future integration task.

M4 protocol result row interfaces are mirrored narrowly in client-core because protocol currently exposes query kinds and command DTOs but not all query result DTO types. Structural tests cover the projection fixture, but a future protocol export/contract test would reduce drift risk.
requested_action: Lead may integrate the accepted M4 client campaign planning UI after verifying the QA handoff and normal integration gates.
